dataset_title: FarmBlock Target Cities — Top 50 Historically Black Food-Insecure Communities

scoring_dimensions: Redlining legacy (NCRC graded D or C)
data_sources: Feeding America Map the Meal Gap 2024
HUD Distressed Cities Technical Assistance Program

top_50_cities.notes: Blackest major city in America. Water infrastructure crisis. Multiple HBCUs. Extreme food desert. Anchor: Jackson State, Tougaloo College.
Most famous food desert in America per researchers. Deep redlining legacy. Anchor: Wayne County Community College, churches, NAACP Detroit.
Civil rights epicenter. Birmingham-Southern, Miles College, Lawson State (HBCUs). Severe food desert in north/west Birmingham. Legacy of industrial redlining.
#1 most food-desert-affected major city in US by population share. LeMoyne-Owen College (HBCU). High poverty concentration in north/south Memphis.
Southwest Georgia. Dougherty County. Albany State University (HBCU on-site). One of highest poverty rates among Black-majority mid-size cities. COVID hit hard.
Post-industrial collapse. Lake County. One of most distressed DCI zip codes nationally. Steel mill legacy. Anchor: churches, Gary Community School Corp.
Water crisis city. Genesee County. Extreme DCI distress. Urban agriculture movement already active post-crisis. Strong community organization base.
Coppin State, Morgan State, Bowie State nearby. Extreme food desert in west/east Baltimore. Redlining maps publicly available. Lower broadband gap than peers.
Post-Katrina food access collapse in Lower 9th Ward. Xavier, Dillard, Southern (HBCUs). Strong anchor institutions. Tourism economy masks deep inequality.
Southern University (HBCU on-site). North Baton Rouge = severe food desert. Environmental justice issues along Cancer Alley. Strong church networks.
Caddo Parish. Grambling State nearby. Northwest Louisiana. Community Development Financial Institutions active. Strong church base.
Civil rights capital. Alabama State University (HBCU). Multiple food deserts in west/south Montgomery. Tourism economy does not benefit Black residents proportionally.
Savannah State University (HBCU). Coastal Georgia. Tourism economy. West Savannah = deep food desert. Strong nonprofit sector.
Paine College (HBCU). Richmond County. Augusta is bifurcated — Masters Golf wealth vs. deep Black poverty. Anchor: churches, Augusta Urban Ministries.
Fort Valley State, Wesleyan College nearby. Bibb County. Middle Georgia. High concentration of food deserts in north Macon. Historic churches.
Muscogee County. Fort Benning adjacent. Black population concentrated in north Columbus — food desert. TSYS/Aflac HQ but economic benefits poorly distributed.
Stillman College (HBCU). UA campus masks poverty. West Tuscaloosa = severe food desert. Tornado history amplifies food insecurity.
Extreme poverty. Historic civil rights city. No full-service grocery store for extended periods. Anchor: churches, Selma University (HBCU). SMALL but critical symbolically.
One of most distressed DCI cities nationally. Across from Philadelphia. School district collapsed and rebuilt. Strong anchor: Cooper University Health, Rutgers-Camden.
Delaware County. Extreme food desert. One of Pennsylvania's most distressed communities. Lincoln University (HBCU) nearby. Anchor: Chester Community Development Corp.
Extreme poverty. Highest Black percentage of any metro-adjacent city. Environmental justice epicenter (toxic contamination). Anchor: East St. Louis Community College.
Los Angeles County. Black + Latino majority. Legacy of redlining and disinvestment. South LA food desert zone. Compton College. Cultural capital for Black America.
LAPD 77th Street Division area. South LA. Extreme food desert. 1965 uprising legacy. Strong grassroots food sovereignty movement. Anchor: Watts Healthcare Corp.
SoFi Stadium wealth vs. displacement. Black residents being pushed out. Historical food access gaps. Anchor: churches, Brotherhood Crusade.
Charleston County. Benedict College, Claflin nearby. I-26 corridor food access gap. SC State nearby. Police violence flashpoint (Walter Scott).
Benedict College, Claflin University (HBCUs). Richland County. North Columbia food desert. State capital — policy leverage point.
SC State University + Claflin University — two HBCUs in one small city. Orangeburg Massacre 1968. Deep poverty. Strong church network. Small but HBCU density unmatched.
FAMU (HBCU flagship). Leon County. South and northwest Tallahassee food desert zones. State capital. Strong FAMU agriculture program — natural FarmBlock anchor.
Alachua County. East Gainesville = historic Black neighborhood, severe food desert. UF campus masks poverty. Anchor: Greater Bethel AME, Gainesville Community Reinvestment Area.
Escambia County. Northwest Pensacola Black neighborhoods. Food access gap in Brownsville district. Anchor: churches, Pensacola State College.
Mobile County. Extreme poverty. Municipality went bankrupt. No pension. Anchor institutions collapsed. One of most distressed small cities in the South.
Washington County. Mississippi Delta. Historic cotton economy legacy. Extreme food insecurity. Delta State nearby. Anchor: churches, Greenville Housing Authority.
Coahoma County. Blues heritage. Mississippi Delta. Extreme poverty. Coahoma Community College (HBCU). No full-service grocery in urban core. Deepest food desert in Delta.
Adams County. Alcorn State University nearby. Historic plantation economy. Natchez has Alcorn proximity — strong HBCU agriculture connection.
Phillips County AR. Delta. Extreme poverty. Food desert. One of poorest counties in the US. Arkansas Delta similar fingerprint to Pine Bluff.
St. Francis County AR. Arkansas Delta. Food desert. Strong church network. Similar Delta profile to Pine Bluff.
Crittenden County. Across from Memphis TN. Food access gap amplified by lack of bridge infrastructure investment. Gateway to Delta food system.
Texas-Arkansas border. Bowie County. Texas College (HBCU) in nearby Tyler. Black community in south Texarkana = food desert. Cross-state jurisdiction adds complexity.
Jefferson County. Lamar University. North and south Beaumont food deserts. Petrochemical industry legacy. Environmental justice + food access convergence.
Jefferson County. Lamar State College. Cancer Alley of Texas — industrial corridor. Post-Harvey recovery. One of most economically distressed Gulf Coast cities.
Lower Black population % but Black community concentrated in food desert zones. Midwest State University. North Texas. NOTE: lower Black % — boundary case.
Tuskegee University — birthplace of Black agricultural science. Macon County. Extreme poverty despite HBCU presence. HIGHEST symbolic value of any city for FarmBlock. Booker T Washington legacy.
Lee County. Auburn adjacent. Black community in east Opelika = food desert. Auburn Univ agriculture program 5 miles away — collaboration leverage.
Calhoun County. PCB contamination legacy (Monsanto). Environmental justice epicenter. Food desert. Jacksonville State nearby. Anchor: churches.
New Hanover County. Wilmington 1898 massacre — original Black political coup. North side food desert. UNCW. Anchor: St. Stephen AME, Cape Fear Community.
NC Central (HBCU). Bull City. East Durham = food desert despite Research Triangle wealth. Hayti historic Black district. Boundary case — income higher but inequality extreme.
Wayne County NC. East NC Black Belt. Seymour Johnson AFB adjacent. North Goldsboro food desert. Anchor: churches, Wayne Community College.
Edgecombe/Nash counties. NC Black Belt. North Carolina Wesleyan, NC Wesleyan nearby. Strong food desert designation in west Rocky Mount. Tobacco legacy economy.

Virginia State University (HBCU on-site). Petersburg is a national model for what happens when post-industrial Black cities lose anchor institutions. VSU agriculture program = immediate FarmBlock partner.

Virginia Union University (HBCU). Original Confederate capital. Church Hill, Gilpin Court food deserts. Strong nonprofit sector. Confronting Confederate monument legacy. Boundary case — income skewed by VCU corridor.

tier_1_immediate.description: Highest fingerprint match to Liberty City + Pine Bluff. Extreme poverty, food desert, HBCU on-site or adjacent, broadband gap. Immediate FarmBlock deployment candidates.

tier_2_strong.description: Strong match. Food desert confirmed, majority Black, poverty 25%+, HBCU nearby. High FarmBlock + BDI potential.

tier_3_strategic.description: Major metro or state capital. Lower poverty rate but extreme inequality within Black community. High policy leverage + HBCU partnership value.

grant_amplification.google_gic_scalability_note: Dataset enables Google.org application to scale from 2 cities (Liberty City + Pine Bluff) to 50 cities representing 5.2M people. All data CC0 and publicly citable.
